title: lasiochlamys hurlimannii
intro: Lasiochlamys hurlimannii, also known as the 'Australian hakea' or 'Hurlimann's lasiochlamys', is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It has been traditionally used in Australian bush medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Lasiochlamys hurlimannii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its purported healing properties.
